title: juglans mandshurica f. rugosa
intro: Juglans mandshurica f. rugosa, also known as the Manchurian walnut, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its broad, elliptical leaves and edible nuts. The bark, leaves, and nuts of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Juglans mandshurica f. rugosa include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies, such as ointments and poultices.
